statement.en: Nemobot Games: Crafting Strategic AI Gaming Agents for Interactive Learning with Large Language Models
statement.summary: This paper introduces a new paradigm for AI game programming, leveraging large language models (LLMs) to extend and operationalize Claude Shannon's taxonomy of game-playing machines. Central to this paradigm is Nemobot, an interactive agentic engineering environment that enables users to create, customize, and deploy LLM-powered game agents while actively engaging with AI-driven strategies. The LLM-based chatbot, integrated within Nemobot, demonstrates its capabilities across four distinct classes of games. For dictionary-based games, it compresses state-action mappings into efficient, generalized models for rapid adaptability. In rigorously solvable games, it employs mathematical reasoning to compute optimal strategies and generates human-readable explanations for its decisions. For heuri

proposer: Chee Wei Tan, Yuchen Wang, Shangxin Guo

reiTyping.dfumt8Reason: Paper describes a novel 2026-dated system (Nemobot) integrating LLMs with game-theoretic frameworks. The work is provisional/preprint status: claims are empirically testable but validation incomplete. The paradigm itself (extending Shannon's taxonomy via LLM agents) is innovative but not yet widely peer-validated. Core technical claims about state compression, strategy generation, and human-readability are contingent on implementation details not fully specified in abstract.
